CIPHER.501.cipherString: Every great mistake has a halfway moment, a split second when it can be recalled and perhaps remedied.
CIPHER.501.question: Someone passed you a piece of paper with this Aristocrat encoded quote by Pearl S. Buck. What does it say?
CIPHER.501.author: Pearl S. Buck

CIPHER.484.cipherString: The end did justify the means sometimes, otherwise nothing would ever be accomplished.
CIPHER.484.question: You receive this cryptic message from the future through an online time capsule. It's a quote by Steve Perry (author), written in a Complete Columnar. The quote has MEANS somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.484.author: Steve Perry (author)

CIPHER.477.cipherString: Power always thinks it has a great soul and vast views beyond the comprehension of the weak.
CIPHER.477.question: A weathered note is found inside an old science textbook. The writing appears to be a quote by John Adams encoded as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.477.author: John Adams

CIPHER.488.cipherString: Success is not to be pursued, it is to be attracted by the person you become.
CIPHER.488.question: Found taped under a desk in study hall: this Checkerboard puzzle by Jim Rohn with a Polybius key of PYRAMIDS. What does it say?
CIPHER.488.author: Jim Rohn

CIPHER.492.question: Upon searching a room, the following were found on scraps of paper. You realize it's encoded as a Cryptarithm. What do the values 105 7896 4236 decode to?

CIPHER.475.cipherString: We must fall back upon the old axiom that when all other contingencies fail, whatever remains, however improbable, must be the truth.
CIPHER.475.question: A famous phrase by Sherlock Holmes has been encoded as a Patristocrat cipher using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher starts with WEM. What does it say?
CIPHER.475.author: Sherlock Holmes

CIPHER.474.cipherString: A single conversation with a wise man is better than ten years of study.
CIPHER.474.question: Hidden inside a fortune cookie was a Chinese proverb encoded using a Nihilist Substitution cipher with a keyword length of 5. The 34th through 43rd cipher units (66 57 90 78 36 85 79 78 46 54) decode to be BETTERTHAN. What does it say?
CIPHER.474.author: Chinese proverb

CIPHER.499.cipherString: Without big data, you are blind and deaf and in the middle of a freeway.
CIPHER.499.question: You stumble across this encoded text on an old floppy disk labeled “Project Discovery.” It's a quote by Geoffrey Moore encoded as a Porta using a keyword of ALBUMS. What does it say?
CIPHER.499.author: Geoffrey Moore

CIPHER.480.question: A phrase has been encoded using the Cryptarithm Cipher. What do the values 1033 85697 3024 decode to?

CIPHER.473.cipherString: El Parlamento mismo no existiría en su forma actual si la gente no hubiera desafiado la ley.
CIPHER.473.question: A quote by Arthur Scargill in Spanish has been encoded using the Xenocrypt Cipher using a K2 alphabet for you to decode. What does it say?
CIPHER.473.author: Arthur Scargill
CIPHER.473.translation: Parliament itself would not exist in its present form had people not defied the law.

CIPHER.489.cipherString: Romance is the poetry of literature.
CIPHER.489.question: This code is found written in chalk on the sidewalk outside the science building. It's a quote by Madame Necker, written in a Baconian Cipher. You are told that the deciphered text starts with ROMA. What does it say?
CIPHER.489.author: Madame Necker

CIPHER.496.cipherString: To shine your brightest light is to be who you truly are.
CIPHER.496.question: Your lava lamp started bubbling in Nihilist Substitution code by Roy T. Bennett with a keyword of NEURAL and Polybius key of EDUCATING. What does it say?
CIPHER.496.author: Roy T. Bennett

CIPHER.476.cipherString: When someone says to me, no one's done that before, I say: great, then I can be the first.
CIPHER.476.question: The message below was encoded into the DNA sequence of a synthetic organism. It translates to a quote by Jonas Deichmann, encrypted using a Complete Columnar Cipher. The quote has SDONE somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.476.author: Jonas Deichmann

CIPHER.494.cipherString: Sometimes small things can become very large, indeed.
CIPHER.494.question: A message flashes briefly on a malfunctioning computer screen. It turns out to be a quote by Rick Riordan written in a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of RULING and Polybius key of RESTOCKING. What does it say?
CIPHER.494.author: Rick Riordan

CIPHER.479.cipherString: After weeks of indoor work it actually felt good to be doing something--clean, direct, muscles and mind.
CIPHER.479.question: Your robot vacuum just projected this message onto the ceiling. It's a quote by Gregory Benford encoded with an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.479.author: Gregory Benford

CIPHER.486.cipherString: Diagnosis is not the end, but the beginning of practice.
CIPHER.486.question: You find a coded sequence scratched into the surface of a lab stool. It turns out to be a quote by Martin H. Fischer, written in a Baconian. What does it say?
CIPHER.486.author: Martin H. Fischer

CIPHER.490.cipherString: We should regret our mistakes and learn from them, but never carry them forward into the future with us.
CIPHER.490.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote by Lucy Maud Montgomery, encrypted as an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.490.author: Lucy Maud Montgomery

CIPHER.472.cipherString: No man is a hero to his valet.
CIPHER.472.question: Solve this quote by Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel that has been encoded using the Hill Cipher with a keyword of FLEX. What does it say?
CIPHER.472.author: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel

CIPHER.487.cipherString: Humanity needs dreams to be able to survive the miseries of daily existence, even if only for an instant.
CIPHER.487.question: Someone left this encrypted quote scribbled on the chalkboard before class began. It's a quote by Oscar Niemeyer, written as an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains the word TO twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.487.author: Oscar Niemeyer

CIPHER.493.cipherString: It isn't given to very many men to have their dreams come true in their lifetimes.
CIPHER.493.question: A message in a bottle washed up in the school's decorative fountain—decode this Checkerboard cipher text by Algis Budrys. The 7th through 13th cipher units (IW NG SW SN NO EG PR) decode to be GIVENTO. What does it say?
CIPHER.493.author: Algis Budrys

CIPHER.483.cipherString: Nos divertimos sólo en nuestro trabajo, en nuestro hacer; Y lo mejor que hacemos es lo que más disfrutamos.
CIPHER.483.question: Someone taped a piece of paper with this sequence onto the back of a trophy from last year's Science Olympiad. It's a quote by Jacobi in Spanish, written using a Xenocrypt Cipher using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.483.author: Jacobi
CIPHER.483.translation: We enjoy ourselves only in our work, our doing; and our best doing is our best enjoyment.

CIPHER.482.cipherString: Never agree to plastic surgery if the doctor's office is full of portraits by Picasso.
CIPHER.482.question: Your microscope slide prep strangely revealed this Checkerboard message with a Polybius key of HYSTERICAL. What does it say?
CIPHER.482.author: Unknown

CIPHER.481.cipherString: Thinking of the sun causes quick beating of my heart -- snowy weather comes on the wind lightly drifting.
CIPHER.481.question: The school's ancient overhead projector flickered and burned this Aristocrat message onto the screen—decode Anna Akhmatova using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.481.author: Anna Akhmatova

CIPHER.497.cipherString: The sun, too, shines into cesspools, and is not polluted.
CIPHER.497.question: An explorer's journal mentions a “hidden truth” encoded below. It turns out to be a quote, written in a Baconian Cipher. What does it say?

CIPHER.485.cipherString: One does not discover new lands without consenting to lose sight of the shore for a very long time.
CIPHER.485.question: Frozen inside an ice core sample from Antarctica: this Aristocrat message by Andre Gide. You are told that the cipher contains the word NOT. What does it say?
CIPHER.485.author: Andre Gide

CIPHER.498.cipherString: I haven't been able to sleep before entering the house; I'm that thrilled to share the same stage as him. It's an absolute dream come true.
CIPHER.498.question: Etched into the surface of a melted vinyl record, you found this Patristocrat-encrypted quote by Nikki Tamboli using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher ends with RUE. What does it say?
CIPHER.498.author: Nikki Tamboli

CIPHER.495.cipherString: Only when our love for someone exceeds our need for them do we have a shot at a genuine relationship together.
CIPHER.495.question: Someone has left a coded message on the classroom 3D printer's display. You’re told it's a quote by Neil Strauss, written in an Aristocrat Cipher using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Key Phrase used to encode it?
CIPHER.495.author: Neil Strauss

CIPHER.491.cipherString: If only life could be a little more tender and art a little more robust.
CIPHER.491.question: A coded message appears after you shine a UV light on a classroom poster. It turns out to be a quote by Alan Rickman, encrypted using a Porta. The 27th through 32nd cipher characters (KVFNEX) decode to be RETEND. What does it say?
CIPHER.491.author: Alan Rickman

CIPHER.478.question: The cafeteria receipt printed a Proverb using the Hill cipher with a keyword of NUMBERING. What does it say?
CIPHER.478.author: Proverb

CIPHER.500.cipherString: Success in life is not for those who run fast, but for those who keep running and always on the move.
CIPHER.500.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote by Bangambiki Habyarimana, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.500.author: Bangambiki Habyarimana
